fundamentals.name: Tesla, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive; and Energy Generation and Storage. The company offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty maintenance services and collision, automotive insurance services, as well as part sales and retail merchandise sale. It also provides sedans and sport utility vehicles through direct and used vehicle sales, a network of Tesla Superchargers, and in-app upgrades; purchase financing and leasing services; services for electric vehicles through its company-owned service locations and Tesla mobile service technicians; and vehicle limited warranties and extended service plans. In addition, the company engages in the design, manufacture, installation, sale, and leasing of solar energy generation and energy storage products, and related services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers and utilities through its website, stores, and galleries, as well as through a network of channel partners. Further, it provides services and repairs to its energy product customers, including under warranty and extended service plans; and various financing options to its residential customers; lithium-ion battery energy storage products, such as Powerwall and Megapack; energy generation products, including solar panels and solar roof; self-driving development and artificial intelligence software, vehicle control and infotainment software, and battery and powertrain. The company was formerly known as Tesla Motors, Inc. and changed its name to Tesla, Inc. in February 2017. Tesla, Inc. was incorporated in 2003 and is headquartered in Austin, Texas.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and earnings expansion, but trades at a very high valuation and has modest margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of $0.41 vs $0.35 expected; next earnings date is 2026-07-22.
positives: Revenue growth 15.8% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 16.6% indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Analyst growth estimates are robust at 13.5% for the current quarter and 23.4% for the current fiscal year.
Strong free cash flow of $5.25B (TTM) and substantial total cash of $44.74B provide financial flexibility.
Current ratio of 2.04 and quick ratio of 1.43 suggest good short-term liquidity.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 386.60 and Forward P/E of 167.91 indicate an extremely high valuation.
Gross margin at 19.1%, operating margin at 4.2%, and profit margin at 3.9% are relatively low for the company's valuation.
Return on equity at 4.9% and return on assets at 2.2% are modest.
High Debt/Equity ratio of 18.74, though offset by significant cash reserves.

news.paragraph: Tesla's stock is experiencing mixed signals, testing short-term moving averages amidst reports of surging Scandinavian demand, while also slipping overnight due to concerns comparing SpaceX's IPO hype to the dot-com bubble. The company was also mentioned as joining the $1 trillion market cap club alongside Meta and Broadcom, but faces diverging opinions on growth and has seen its stock decline while the broader market improves. The recent earnings report beat expectations, with analysts projecting solid growth for the current quarter and fiscal year.

headlines.title: Tesla Stock Tests Short-Term Moving Averages Amid Surging Scandinavian Demand
TSLA Stock Slips Overnight: SpaceX IPO Hype Is Starting To Look Like Amazon's Dot-Com Bubble Peak In 2000, Warns Ex-Lehman Trader
